_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
RoleSwitcher is a MudSelect dropdown that simulates role switching for demo purposes. It allows developers to toggle between three officer roles—Reception, Population, and Police Clerk—by calling `ICurrentOfficer.SetRole()`. This component is part of the registration feature because it provides a way to demonstrate different user roles within the application.

It is actually rendered in `MainLayout.razor` as a global app-bar component, not on registration pages. Its placement under `Features/Registration` is purely historical from Phase 0, when that folder bundled registration pages, fake authentication, and the role switcher together. It does not logically belong there.

The component belongs in `Auth/` or `Components/Layout/` instead. It is a dev tooling and shell concern, not registration UI. The roles it simulates are registration workflow roles, but the switcher itself is not part of that flow. It should eventually be removed once real authentication is implemented.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Your instinct is right: **`RoleSwitcher` is not really a Registration feature component** — it ended up there because of how Phase 0 was organized, not because it belongs to registration UI.

## What it actually is

`RoleSwitcher` is a **fake-auth dev tool**. It renders a dropdown so you can switch between three demo officer roles:

```1:23:src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Components/RoleSwitcher.razor
@using SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web.Auth
@inject ICurrentOfficer CurrentOfficer

<div class="role-switcher">
    <MudSelect T="OfficerRole"
               Value="CurrentOfficer.Role"
               ValueChanged="OnRoleChanged"
               ...
        <MudSelectItem Value="OfficerRole.ReceptionOfficer">Reception officer</MudSelectItem>
        <MudSelectItem Value="OfficerRole.PopulationOfficer">Population officer</MudSelectItem>
        <MudSelectItem Value="OfficerRole.PoliceClerk">Police clerk</MudSelectItem>
    </MudSelect>
</div>

@code {
    private void OnRoleChanged(OfficerRole role)
    {
        CurrentOfficer.SetRole(role);
    }
}
```

The real auth logic lives under `Auth/` (`ICurrentOfficer`, `CurrentOfficer`, `OfficerRole`), not in Registration.

## Where it’s used

It is **not** used on registration pages. It’s wired into the **global app shell**:

```18:24:src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor
    <AppTopNavigation Title="Schaerbeek — Population Department"
                      OfficerDisplayName="@CurrentOfficer.DisplayName"
                      OnMenuToggle="ToggleDrawer">
        <TrailingContent>
            <RoleSwitcher />
        </TrailingContent>
    </AppTopNavigation>
```

So it’s an app-bar concern, like the officer name and side nav — not a registration-specific widget.

## Why it’s under `Features/Registration`

Phase 0 was built as the **first vertical slice: Registration**. That slice bundled together:

- Registration handlers and pages  
- Fake auth (`ICurrentOfficer`)  
- `RoleSwitcher` in the app bar  

The project docs reflect that layout explicitly:

```
Web/Features/Registration/
├── ...
├── Components/
│   └── RoleSwitcher.razor
```

So it’s **historical folder placement** from “everything we built in Phase 0 lives under Registration,” not a sign that the component is registration-specific.

## Why it was introduced at all

The roles *are* tied to the registration workflow:

- Handlers use `ICurrentOfficer` (e.g. who opened a case, who attached a document)
- `CanApproveRegistration` is only true for `PopulationOfficer`
- `MainLayout` passes the current role into `AppSideNavigation` for role-filtered nav items

The switcher exists so you can **demo those role-based behaviors without real login**. The design docs describe it as temporary fake auth that will eventually be replaced by real authentication.

## Where it would fit better

Conceptually, `RoleSwitcher` belongs with:

- **`Auth/`** (it only talks to `ICurrentOfficer`), or  
- **`Components/Layout/`** (it only appears in `MainLayout`)

By contrast, `DocumentUpload.razor` and `NewCaseDialog.razor` in the same folder *are* registration-specific — those placements make sense.

**Bottom line:** It’s in `Features/Registration` because Phase 0 grouped fake auth with the first registration slice, even though the component is global shell/auth tooling, not part of the registration feature itself. Moving it to `Auth/` or `Components/Layout/` would better match how it’s actually used.

---

_**User**_

Can we move it where it should be? Can you also check that we have a correct architecture until now?

Moving RoleSwitcher to `Auth/Components/RoleSwitcher.razor`.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Moving files and updating references. I considered updating the architecture document, but I will skip it since the user did not request markdown edits.

I will update phase-0-foundation.md. The document shows the wrong folder structure and would mislead. This is part of verifying the architecture.

Writing RoleSwitcher.razor and its CSS file. Deleting the old files. Updating MainLayout and adding the Auth component namespace to Components/_Imports.razor. Removing the old registration components import from MainLayout. The architecture document will get only a minimal path update.

The overall architecture is sound. The VSA structure, domain separation, and repository injection patterns are correct. The Auth folder properly houses cross-cutting concerns like RoleSwitcher. Handlers are manually registered in Program.cs without MediatR, which is acceptable. The DesignSystem remains separate from Features. I will run tests after completing the moves.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The architecture looks good. RoleSwitcher was moved to Auth/Components/. Auth/_Imports.razor was added.

MainLayout and Components/_Imports.razor were updated. phase-0-foundation.md was revised. Old files were deleted.

_**Agent (default)**_

`RoleSwitcher` is now under `Auth/Components/`, where it belongs with the rest of the fake-auth tooling. Build and all 36 tests pass.

## What changed

| Before | After |
|--------|-------|
| `Features/Registration/Components/RoleSwitcher.razor` | `Auth/Components/RoleSwitcher.razor` |
| Imported via `Features.Registration.Components` in `MainLayout` | Imported via `Auth.Components` in `Components/_Imports.razor` |

Also added `Auth/_Imports.razor` (MudBlazor usings for auth components) and updated the folder diagram in `docs/phases/phase-0-foundation.md`.

`Features/Registration/Components/` now only holds registration-specific UI: `DocumentUpload.razor` and `NewCaseDialog.razor`.

## Architecture review

Overall the structure matches what `ARCHITECTURE.md` describes. Here's the breakdown:

### Solid — keep as-is

**Project boundaries** — dependency chain is correct:
```
AppHost → Web → ServiceDefaults, Domain, Infrastructure
Infrastructure → Domain
Domain → (nothing)
```

**Vertical slices** — each use case is self-contained (`OpenRegistrationCase/`, `RecordIdentity/`, etc.) with endpoint, handler, request, validator. `RegistrationEndpoints.cs` groups routes under `/api/registration` as documented.

**Handlers** — thin orchestrators: validate → load aggregate → domain method → persist → DTO. Example:

```7:36:src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/OpenRegistrationCase/OpenRegistrationCaseHandler.cs
public sealed class OpenRegistrationCaseHandler(
    IRegistrationCaseRepository repository,
    ICurrentOfficer currentOfficer,
    ...
```

**Blazor + API sharing logic** — pages inject handlers directly (no unnecessary HTTP in Server mode), which matches the documented convention.

**Layer separation**:
- `Domain/` — aggregates, value objects, repository interfaces (no EF/ASP.NET)
- `Infrastructure/` — EF configs, repositories, migrations
- `DesignSystem/` — reusable UI primitives (separate from feature UI)
- `Auth/` — cross-cutting fake auth (`ICurrentOfficer`, roles, now `RoleSwitcher`)
- `Features/Registration/` — bounded-context slices + shared registration components

**Composition root** — `Program.cs` registers infrastructure, validators, handlers, and auth in one place. Manual handler registration is intentional (no MediatR yet).

### Fixed today

- **`RoleSwitcher` in Registration** — was the one clear misplacement; it’s global auth UI, not registration feature UI.

### Minor notes (not problems, just awareness)

1. **`Program.cs` handler registration** — each new slice needs a line here. Fine for now; consider source-generated or assembly scanning only if this grows painful.

2. **`AppSideNavigation` takes `OfficerRole`** — design system nav knows about auth roles for filtering. Acceptable for a single app; if roles proliferate, pass pre-filtered items from `MainLayout` instead.

3. **`DesignSystemShowcase`** lives in `Components/Pages/` (dev-only) — correct; not a feature slice.

4. **`AttachDocument` disables antiforgery** — intentional for multipart file uploads from Blazor; worth keeping an eye on when real auth lands.

5. **Docs** — design-system docs still refer to `RoleSwitcher` by name (still valid); only the path in `phase-0-foundation.md` needed updating. `ARCHITECTURE.md` could optionally document `Auth/Components/` under cross-cutting concerns when you next touch that doc.

### Current Web layout (mental model)

```
Web/
├── Auth/              ← fake auth services + RoleSwitcher UI
├── Components/        ← app shell (MainLayout, Routes, Home)
├── DesignSystem/      ← reusable UI kit
├── Features/
│   └── Registration/  ← vertical slices + registration-only components
└── Validation/        ← shared validation helpers
```

Nothing else looks out of place for the current phase. The architecture is coherent and ready for the next registration slices.
